title: wollastonia urticifolia
intro: Wollastonia urticifolia, also known as the itch grass or itch weed, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids in its leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Wollastonia urticifolia have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
